primitives_found.note: EB5 claimed PRIMITIVE_MISSING for LDS-alloc — INCORRECT. AddrSpace.LOCAL and UOp.barrier are already used in production code in qk_flash_decode.py.

cross_kernel_blocker: AMD HIP LDS is workgroup-scoped; lifetime ends at kernel boundary. Producer (E_49152_32_3) and consumer (flash_partial) are separate kernel launches. No cross-kernel LDS sharing exists on gfx1100.
per_token_v_lds_reason: Per-token V LDS staging (same pattern as K in flash_pall_lds_crosslane): REACHABLE_NOW in UOp terms but has ZERO benefit. V access is V[kv, t, d] where d is the thread index — each thread reads one element per token, no cross-thread data reuse. Staging into LDS adds write+barrier overhead with no read reuse.
full_split_v_lds_blocker: Full-split V staging (L=128 tokens × Hd=128 = 32KB per workgroup) would eliminate global V reads in flash_partial and provide stronger warming than L2. But it requires a 'prologue range' loop that executes BEFORE the main j REDUCE loop: (1) all W=129 threads cooperatively load V[kv, s*L..s*L+L, :] into LDS, (2) barrier, (3) j REDUCE reads from LDS. The current UOp kernel DSL supports REDUCE ranges but has no 'pre-stage range' primitive that runs before the main accumulation loop. The lifecycle kernel (flash_pall_score_state_pv_lifecycle) also reads V direct from global with no LDS staging, confirming this pattern is not currently expressible.
corrected_classification: EMITTER_BLOCKED (was PRIMITIVE_MISSING in EB5 — EB5 was wrong; primitives exist, the structural pattern is what's missing)
reopen_condition: A 'prologue range' UOp primitive that allows a pre-stage loop before the main REDUCE loop, writing a tensor tile into LDS with an implicit post-stage barrier. Once this pattern is supported, flash_partial_coop_vec can be modified to cooperatively load its full V split into LDS before the accumulation reduce, eliminating E_49152_32_3 and providing superior warming to the current L2-copy approach.

lds_sizing.occupancy_impact: 2 workgroups/CU max (vs. potentially 4+ without V LDS), but net benefit from eliminating E_49152_32_3 (6.69%) likely outweighs occupancy reduction

skip_reason: LDS0 found EMITTER_BLOCKED on the only viable angle (full-split V staging). No phase run required per track spec.
